message:
chore(beads): add issue bd-1g3sg for UBS reservation pattern handling

Tracked a new bug discovered during development: UBS notifications should
honor ** glob reservation patterns. The issue is currently in_progress with
priority 2 and links back to bd-1da61 as its discovery source.

This is a routine beads issue tracker sync maintaining the project's work
item state for multi-agent coordination visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1g1v","title":"Task: Create assign.go command","description":"## Purpose
Implement ntm assign command for intelligent task-to-agent assignment.

## Command
ntm assign myproject [--auto] [--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency]

## Command Flow
1. Query BV: bv --robot-triage for prioritized work
2. Query NTM: Get idle agents and their types/context usage
3. Run assignment algorithm
4. Display recommendations or execute

## Output
Task Assignment Recommendations for myproject
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Ready Work (from BV):
  bd-42 [P1] Refactor authentication module
  bd-45 [P2] Add unit tests for API

Available Agents:
  cc_1 (Claude/Opus) - 34% context, idle 5m
  cod_1 (Codex) - 45% context, idle 8m

Recommended Assignments:
  cc_1 -> bd-42 (refactoring=0.95)
  cod_1 -> bd-47 (bug fix=0.90)

Execute all? [y/N]

## Acceptance Criteria
- Command shows recommendations
- --auto executes without confirmation
- Strategy flag affects assignment
- Graceful when BV unavailable

## Technical Notes
Create internal/cli/assign.go
Integrate with bv --robot-triage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:04.350484504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:43.803824037Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:43.803747402Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm assign command with text UI, JSON output, multiple strategies (balanced/speed/quality/dependency), interactive confirmation, and auto mode","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g1v","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g2by","title":"Task: Implement finding-to-bead export command","description":"## Implementation
Add `ntm ensemble export-findings` command:

1. Load findings from last run or `--run-id`.
2. Interactive selector for findings.
3. For each selected finding, call `br create` with:
   - Title from finding thesis
   - Body includes provenance + evidence
   - Priority mapped from severity
4. Support `--all` and `--dry-run` preview.

## Logging
- Log selected finding IDs + mapped priority
- Log `br create` results (bead IDs)

## Tests
- Unit: severity→priority mapping
- Integration: stub br runner
- E2E: dry-run + real create in temp .beads project

## Dependencies
- OutputSchema (bd-1wnxl)
- Provenance tracking (bd-364w3)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:18:03.942744314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:49:20.011924236Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:49:20.011709852Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g2by","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g2by","depends_on_id":"bd-364w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g2by","depends_on_id":"bd-37ge8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g3f","title":"Unit Tests: Smart staggering timing logic","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Test Suite for Smart Staggering

### Test Coverage Required
1. **StaggerScheduler tests** (internal/stagger/schedule_test.go)
   - Interval calculation
   - Agent ordering
   - Dependency-aware scheduling
   - Maximum parallelism limits

2. **PromptQueue tests** (internal/stagger/queue_test.go)

   - Priority insertion
   - Delay timing
   - Cancel/reschedule

3. **ResourceMonitor tests** (internal/stagger/resource_test.go)
   - API rate limit detection
   - Memory pressure detection
   - Adaptive delay adjustment

### Test Data
- Various agent counts (1-10)
- Different stagger intervals
- Simulated rate limit responses

### Logging

 {"id":"bd-1g5t8","title":"Task: Extend balanced strategy with assignment tracking","description":"## Overview
Balanced strategy already exists. This task extends it to use **real assignment tracking data** (from `bd-92e1`) and improves tie‑breakers without changing the public strategy name.

## Why
Current balanced behavior assumes assignment counts are populated. Without tracking, all counts are zero, so the strategy degenerates to simple capability scoring. We want true load‑aware balancing once tracking is available.

## Changes (extend, don’t replace)
- Wire assignment tracking into `assign.Agent.Assignments` (live counts per agent).
- Add optional tie‑breakers:
  1) fewer active assignments
  2) idle status
  3) least‑recent assignment timestamp
  4) best capability score
- Keep deterministic ordering for identical inputs.

## Inputs
- Assignment tracker state (`bd-92e1`).
- Agent health/availability (if available in current context).

## Outputs
- Deterministic assignment map (pane -> bead).
- Robot output includes per‑agent load snapshot and reasons (if present).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Balanced strategy still returns consistent assignments for the same inputs.
- Assignment counts reflect real state when tracking is enabled.
- Graceful fallback when tracking data is unavailable.
- Tests updated to cover load‑aware tie‑breakers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:40.168441307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660098480Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660054547Z","close_reason":"Implemented load-aware balanced strategy with tie-breakers: 1) fewer active assignments, 2) capability score, 3) least-recently assigned, 4) lower pane index for determinism. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g9wv","title":"Feature: Robot Status Enrichments","description":"## Overview

Enrich the existing `ntm --robot-status` output with critical operational data that
is currently missing but essential for automated swarm management.

## Background

The current --robot-status returns basic pane information (index, agent_type, is_active)
but lacks the data needed to:
- Detect rate-limited panes
- Identify hung processes
- Know which PID to kill for restart
- Track output freshness for stuck detection

## Proposed Schema Addition

Each pane object in the status output should include:

```json
{
  "index": 2,
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "pid": 12345,                    // Shell PID (already available)
  "child_pid": 12346,              // NEW: The actual agent process PID
  "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T...", // NEW: Last output timestamp
  "rate_limit_detected": false,    // NEW: Pattern match on recent output
  "process_state": "running",      // NEW: running|sleeping|zombie|dead
  "memory_mb": 450,                // NEW: Process memory usage
  "output_lines_since_last": 42    // NEW: Activity indicator
}
```

## Justification for Each Field

1. **child_pid**: Required for kill -9 when soft restart fails. Currently operators
   must run `pgrep -P <shell_pid>` manually.

2. **last_output_ts**: Enables timeout-based stuck detection without external state.
   "No output for 5 minutes" = likely stuck.

3. **rate_limit_detected**: Pattern match for "You've hit your limit" or similar.
   This is the #1 failure mode in practice. Must be detected programmatically.

4. **process_state**: From /proc/<pid>/status. Zombie processes need different handling
   than running ones.

5. **memory_mb**: Detect runaway processes consuming excessive memory.

6. **output_lines_since_last**: Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing.

## Implementation Notes

- child_pid: Use pgrep -P or parse /proc/<pid>/task
- last_output_ts: Track during capture-pane operations
- rate_limit_detected: Grep last N lines of pane output
- process_state: Read /proc/<pid>/status State field
- memory_mb: Read /proc/<pid>/status VmRSS field
- These should be gathered efficiently (single pass where possible)

## Performance Considerations

- Status is called frequently; keep it fast
- Pattern matching for rate limits should be bounded (last 50 lines)
- Process state reads are cheap (procfs)
- Consider caching with short TTL if needed

## Testing Requirements

- Unit tests for each field extraction
- E2E tests with actual stuck/rate-limited agents
- Performance benchmarks for large swarms (20+ panes)","notes":"Robot Status Enrichments feature is complete. All essential fields implemented: rate_limit_detected (bd-1mcvs), child_pid (bd-36dd7), last_output_ts (bd-3hxdl), process_state (bd-3ivl8), memory_mb (bd-lo0ko). The output_lines_since_last field (bd-33tuj) was deliberately skipped for performance reasons per existing comment in status_enrich.go.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:13:19.647496277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086289872Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086191316Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g9wv","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gbt5","title":"Add ntm assign --reassign for moving bead between agents","description":"## Overview
Allow users to move an assignment from one agent to another without losing tracking state.

## Use Cases
1. Agent is stuck and user wants to give bead to a faster agent
2. User realizes a bead is better suited for a different agent type
3. Load balancing - redistribute work mid-session

## Usage
```bash
# Reassign bead to a different pane
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Reassign to specific agent type (picks idle agent of that type)
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-type=codex

# Reassign with new prompt
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4 --prompt="Continue from where the previous agent left off"
```

## Workflow
1. Validate bead is currently assigned
2. Get current assignment info
3. Update assignment status to "reassigned"
4. Create new assignment for target agent
5. Release file reservations from old agent (if any)
6. Create file reservations for new agent (if any)
7. Send prompt to new agent
8. Return reassignment result

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "reassigned": {
      "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 4,
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "agent_name": "BlueLake",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true,
      "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
      "previous_pane": 2,
      "previous_agent": "GreenCastle",
      "previous_agent_type": "claude",
      "previous_status": "working",
      "file_reservations_transferred": true
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "TARGET_BUSY",
    "message": "pane 4 already has assignment bd-abc",
    "details": {
      "current_bead": "bd-abc",
      "current_status": "working"
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- NOT_ASSIGNED: Bead doesn't have an active assignment
- TARGET_BUSY: Target pane already has an assignment
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Target pane doesn't exist
- NO_IDLE_AGENT: No idle agent of specified type

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reassign bead to specific pane
- [ ] Reassign by agent type (auto-select idle)
- [ ] Transfer file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Update assignment store correctly
- [ ] Optional custom prompt
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Clear error codes

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Assignment state update logic
- Unit test: File reservation transfer
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Full reassignment workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:36:52.859683490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854201422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854147310Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
